program_en: B.S.Sc. in Government and Public Administration
program_chi: 政治與行政學社會科學學士
learning_outcomes:
Government and Public Administration 1. Major Programme: Expected learning outcomes: (1) Knowledge Outcome (K): Understanding
of fundamental concepts and intellectual perspectives in political science
and public administration, and the ability to participate and contribute to
current debates in public policies and problems, with both local and global
perspectives. (2) Skills Outcome (S): Mastery of
essential analytical skills, particularly the abilities to engage in
independent and critical thinking, to make coherent and consistent arguments,
and to critically evaluate alternative arguments in a constructive fashion; mastery of bilingual communication skills; competency in qualitative and
quantitative political science methodologies as well as in tackling practical
problems; ability to work as a team leader and a team member; and aptitude to
continue with life-long learning. (3) Value Outcome (V): Awareness of the
importance of politics in daily life and studies; appreciation and tolerance
of different perspectives in understanding politics and interpreting
political phenomena; and a sense of purpose, civic responsibility, global
citizenship, and commitment in life. 2. Minor Programme: Upon the completion of the Minor
Programme, students will have demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental
issues related to the subject, acquired knowledge of some aspects of the
subject with sufficient exposure and at a suitable level to allow further
study in this subject or related subjects or to pursue a related career.
study_scheme:

Public Administration Applicable to
students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete
a minimum of 72 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
GPAD1020 and any two courses, offered by two different
Programmes/Departments/Schools, chosen from the following:
ARCH1001, 1002, COMM1110, 1120, 1150, DSPS1001, 1003,
1004, ECON1210, 1220, 2011, GLSD1001, GRMD1302, 1401, 1402, PSYC1000, 1630,
SOCI1001, 1201, SOSC1001, 1002, SOWK1001, 1113, 1114, URSP1001, 1002
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  24
GPAD1000, 1030, 1050, 1070, 1095, 2111, 2130, 2345
3.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  Field Electives[a] (six courses chosen from two of
the following six fields, with three courses in each field; courses that
appear in more than one field can only be counted towards one field)  |  18
(i)  |  Politics - Comparative Politics
GPAD1016, 1046, 1055, 1056, 2120, 2140, 2145, 2196, 2215,
2300, 2395, 2450, 3040, 3050, 3160, 3161, 3190, 3360, 4080, 4142
(ii)  |  Politics - Greater China
GPAD1066, 1073, 1074, 1077, 2015,
2025, 2350, 2370, 2555, 3260, 4027, 4030, 4065, 4385

GPAD1076, 1100, 2020, 2065, 2075,

(b)  |  Free Major Electives (any five GPAD courses, other than

(c)  |  Capstone Courses (minimum 6 units chosen from the following; cannot be double counted as field electives if courses appear in both field
electives and capstone courses)  |  6
(i)  |  GPAD4601
(ii)  |  GPAD4602
(iii)  |  GPAD4701 and 4702
(iv)  |  GPAD4801
(v)  |  GPAD4802
(vi)  |  Seminar courses at 4000 level:
GPAD4010, 4020, 4027, 4030, 4065, 4077, 4080, 4111,
4142, 4200, 4211, 4225, 4335, 4385
Of the Elective Courses, at least nine courses (27
units), including the two (6 units) capstone courses, should be at 2000 or
above level.
Total:
72
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  GPAD courses at 2000 and
above level will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
[a]  |  The six courses to be used to
fulfill the requirement of Field Electives should not include courses that
have been used to fulfill the requirements in 3(b) and (c).
